description: Daisy and Deke share a lighthearted moment searching for medication and snacks, with Deke reminiscing about his mother and oranges. Daisy maintains her boundaries, clarifying their relationship while Deke tries to build rapport. Their brief connection adds some levity amidst growing danger.

transcript.line: Okay, so, we need to find where they keep the medication. Something for nerve pain.
Whoa. “Orange scented.” That's amazing. Oh, my!
Okay, snack time's over, Huckleberry. Simmons is waiting.
It's nearly impossible to get fresh fruits and vegetables. But e-every year for my birthday, my mom would find me a big, juicy orange.
Yeah, it was. I loved the way my hands smelled after. What about you? Did you have any ‭special birthday traditions growing up?
Seriously? Nothing? Were you just raised in a house by sucky parents?
More like an orphanage with mean, scary nuns.
Oh, I'm... I'm sorry. I didn't mean to be a jerk.
Well, I mean sometimes I do, because I think it's funny, but not just then. I thought ‭we were having a moment.
Just to be clear, you and I have never had a moment. That was just you telling me a story about your very nice mom and an orange. Deke!
